name: Maris Otter
description: Classic UK heritage barley variety. Rich, biscuity, slightly nutty malt character with a fuller mouthfeel than modern pale malts. Iconic base malt for British ales.

name: Pale 2-Row
description: Workhorse American base malt. Very clean, neutral profile that lets hops and yeast express. The default base malt for American craft styles.

name: Pilsner Malt
description: Floor-malted German pilsner. Pale, crisp, gently grainy with a hint of honey. Essential for authentic continental lagers.

name: Vienna Malt
description: Slightly kilned base malt. Adds a subtle toast and bread-crust character without sweetness. Backbone of Vienna lagers and Märzens.

name: Munich Malt (Light)
description: Deeper kilned than Vienna. Rich malt sweetness with a melanoidin backbone. Defining malt of Munich Helles, Dunkel, Märzen.

name: Pale Wheat Malt
description: Adds protein for head retention, body, and a subtle bready/grainy character. Essential for hefeweizens, witbiers, and modern hazy IPAs.

name: Flaked Oats
description: Pre-gelatinised oats. Adds silky body, mouthfeel, and slight haze. Essential for oatmeal stouts and modern hazy IPAs.

name: Crystal Malt 60L
description: Mid-colour crystal/caramel malt. Adds toffee, raisin, and dried-fruit notes. The classic crystal for British and American ambers/IPAs.

name: Crystal Malt 120L
description: Dark crystal malt. Pronounced raisin, plum, and burnt sugar character. Use sparingly for depth in dark ales and old ales.

name: Chocolate Malt
description: Roasted but not quite to roast-barley levels. Cocoa, coffee, and chocolate notes without the sharp astringency of black malt.

name: Roasted Barley
description: Unmalted, kiln-roasted barley. The defining ingredient of Irish stout — sharp coffee bitterness, dry roast finish.

name: Munich Malt (Dark)
description: Deeper, richer Munich. Pronounced melanoidin and toasted bread character. Backbone of Doppelbock and Dunkel.

name: Carapils / Carafoam
description: Dextrin malt. Adds body and head retention without colour or sweetness. Use as a small percentage in any beer needing more mouthfeel.

name: Biscuit Malt
description: Lightly toasted Belgian malt. Pronounced cracker, biscuit, and toast character. Excellent for adding depth to British and Belgian styles.

name: Flaked Wheat
description: Unmalted wheat, gelatinised. Adds protein, head retention, and a subtle grainy tang. Used in Belgian witbiers and modern hazy IPAs.

name: Rye Malt
description: Adds a distinctive spicy, earthy character and improves body. Popular in rye IPAs, roggenbiers, and modern craft ales.

name: Flaked Rye
description: Pre-gelatinised rye. Adds body, a silky mouthfeel, and subtle spicy notes without the haze of rye malt.

name: Melanoidin Malt
description: Intensifies malt aroma and promotes a full, red colour. Adds melanoidin-rich depth to amber lagers, bocks, and Scottish ales.

name: Caramunich III
description: Dark caramel malt with pronounced toffee, dried-fruit, and dark-caramel notes. Common in Bocks, Doppelbocks, and dark lagers.

name: Special B Malt
description: Very dark Belgian caramel malt. Intense raisin, plum, and burnt-sugar character. Signature malt in Belgian Dubbels and Dark Strong Ales.

name: Black Malt
description: Heavily roasted malt. Sharp, astringent, burnt-coffee character. Use sparingly for colour adjustment or intense roast flavour.

name: Acidulated Malt
description: Lactobacillus-soured malt. Used to lower mash pH naturally or add subtle tartness in Berliner Weisse and sour styles.

name: Honey Malt
description: Distinctive honey-like sweetness and aroma. Intensely malty with a rounded, sweet character. Popular in ambers, browns, and specialty ales.

name: Rauchmalz (Beechwood Smoked)
description: Beechwood-smoked barley malt. Pronounced smoky, bacon-like character. The defining malt of Bamberg Rauchbier.

name: Light DME
description: Dry malt extract — light. Clean, neutral base for extract and partial-mash brewing. Very fermentable with minimal colour contribution.

name: Dark LME
description: Liquid malt extract — dark. Caramel and subtle roast notes. Convenient base for porters, stouts, and dark ales using extract methods.

name: Corn Sugar (Dextrose)
description: Highly fermentable simple sugar. Lightens body and boosts ABV without adding flavour or colour. Common in Belgian styles and high-gravity beers.

name: Turbinado Sugar
description: Raw cane sugar with subtle molasses notes. Adds fermentables with a hint of rum-like character. Popular in Belgian Strong Ales.

name: Golden Promise
description: Scottish heritage barley with a sweeter, fuller character than Maris Otter. Slightly nutty with honeyed malt notes. The base malt of many Scottish ales and modern craft breweries.

name: Double Roasted Crystal
description: Intensely flavoured crystal malt. Burnt sugar, dark fruit, and toffee with a rich ruby colour. A little goes a long way in old ales, barleywines, and porters.

name: Carafa Special I
description: De-husked roasted malt. Adds colour and subtle roast/coffee notes without the harsh astringency of black malt. Essential for Schwarzbier and smooth dark lagers.

name: Carafa Special II
description: Medium de-husked roasted malt. Deeper colour and more pronounced dark chocolate notes than Carafa I. Smooth bitterness for Dunkel, Doppelbock, and dark ales.

name: Carafa Special III
description: The darkest de-husked roast malt. Intense colour with espresso and dark chocolate. Use sparingly for colour adjustment or boldly for Imperial Stouts.

name: Victory Malt
description: Biscuity, toasty, and slightly nutty American specialty malt. Adds warm bread crust character without the sweetness of crystal. Excellent in American amber and brown ales.

name: Aromatic Malt
description: Intensely malty Belgian specialty malt. Promotes rich malt aroma and a full, red-amber colour. Common in Belgian Dubbels, Ambers, and Scottish ales.

name: Special Roast
description: Toasty, biscuity, and slightly tangy. Adds complexity and a dry, crisp edge to brown ales, porters, and stouts. Unique tangy character sets it apart from other roast malts.

name: Midnight Wheat
description: De-husked roasted wheat malt. Adds intense colour with smooth, non-astringent roast character. The wheat version of Carafa — perfect for dark wheat beers and schwarzbiers.

name: Flaked Barley
description: Pre-gelatinised barley. Adds body, creamy mouthfeel, and head retention without colour. Essential for Irish stout and any beer needing fuller body.

name: Flaked Corn
description: Pre-gelatinised maize. Lightens body and colour while adding a subtle sweetness. Classic adjunct for Cream Ales, American Lagers, and historical pre-prohibition styles.

name: Lactose
description: Milk sugar — unfermentable by brewer's yeast. Adds sweetness, body, and a creamy mouthfeel. The defining ingredient of Milk Stout and Sweet Stout.

name: Belgian Candi Sugar (Clear)
description: Highly fermentable beet sugar. Boosts ABV without adding colour or flavour. Traditional in Belgian Tripels and Strong Golden Ales for a dry, crisp finish.

name: Belgian Candi Sugar (Dark)
description: Caramelised beet sugar with rich plum, raisin, and toffee notes. Adds colour and complex dark-fruit character to Belgian Dubbels, Dark Strong Ales, and Quadrupels.
